generators.description: Cross-arm bridge arm 0→arm 1: arm_0_d → arm_1_{d+1} [PARITY_BLOCK — active constraint, Type II obstruction]

graph_snapshot.edge_semantics: Perturbation level k=1: cross_link_{0..0} PARITY_BLOCK (all arms [1] lose multi-path access). arm_advance_* and cross_link_{1..3} remain OK-tagged. |M| = 20 - 5*1 = 15. PDI = 15/25 = 0.600. Type II obstruction per blocked cross_link: arm_{(k+1)%4}_d (d>=2) loses its only merge edge -> exits M, stays in R.

invariants.expression: tau(g) = OK for arm_advance_* and cross_link_{1,2,3}; tau(cross_link_{0}) = PARITY_BLOCK
invariants.name: partial_ok_tagging

invariants.expression: join(tau(g_arm), tau(g_cross)) = OK for arms {1,2,3}; join = PARITY_BLOCK for arm(s) {1}
invariants.name: merge_stability_partial

system_metadata.description: Synthetic FLEXIBLE PLANNER grid with k=1 PARITY_BLOCK obstruction(s). Blocked: ['cross_link_0']. PDI=0.600 (delta from baseline: -0.200). Regime: FLEXIBLE PLANNER. EA=0.3750. Demonstrates Bridge Theorem B1: each PARITY_BLOCK tag produces a Type II obstruction removing exactly 5 states from M (arm_[1]).
